compiled_state.title: Create REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE.md — large-diff classification and routing workflow
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## testing_mode: GREEN

## TDD Requirement

Exemption: Rule 5 / criterion 3 — non-executable LLM instruction file. Structural boundary tests in Task c394-c484 provide RED coverage. No behavioral correctness assertions are deterministically testable for a markdown workflow.

## Files to Create

### plugins/dso/docs/workflows/REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE.md

Required sections (6 numbered steps):

## Step 0: Prerequisites
DIFF_HASH and ARTIFACTS_DIR passed from REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md Step 2b diversion caller (the caller already computed these in REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md Steps 1-2).

## Step 1: Sampling
Run: .claude/scripts/dso review-sample-files.sh
If exits 1 (INSUFFICIENT_FILES): set ROUTING=FALLBACK, skip to Step 4.

## Step 2: Pattern Extraction
For each of the 7 sampled files, dispatch haiku sub-agent with file diff excerpt. Agent returns JSON per huge-diff-pattern-extraction.md contract: {transformation_description, before_pattern, after_pattern, confidence}. If ≥2 agent failures: ROUTING=FALLBACK, skip to Step 4.

## Step 3: Consensus Evaluation
Count sampled files with identical transformation_description. If match_count ≥ 5: ROUTING=CONFIRMED_REFACTOR. Else: ROUTING=FALLBACK.

## Step 4: Routing
CONFIRMED_REFACTOR: dispatch standard deep-tier code review (stub — specialized refactor agents pending Stories cf76-7091 / efe7-7f1d).
FALLBACK: dispatch standard deep-tier code review.
Both paths produce reviewer-findings.json and REVIEWER_HASH.

## Step 5: Record Review
"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/record-review.sh" --expected-hash "$DIFF_HASH" --reviewer-hash "$REVIEWER_HASH"
Surface error if non-zero exit.

Contract reference: pattern extraction schema defined in plugins/dso/docs/contracts/huge-diff-pattern-extraction.md

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 6 structural tests pass
  Verify: bash tests/workflows/test-review-workflow-huge.sh 2>&1 | grep -c PASS | grep -q '^6$' && echo PASS
- [ ] Workflow file exists
  Verify: test -f plugins/dso/docs/workflows/REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE.md && echo PASS
- [ ] Referential integrity — references sampling script
  Verify: grep -q 'review-sample-files.sh' plugins/dso/docs/workflows/REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE.md && echo PASS
- [ ] Referential integrity — references pattern contract
  Verify: grep -q 'huge-diff-pattern-extraction.md' plugins/dso/docs/workflows/REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE.md && echo PASS
- [ ] Shim compliance — record-review.sh via CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT
  Verify: grep -q 'CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT.*record-review.sh' plugins/dso/docs/workflows/REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE.md && echo PASS
- [ ] Required flags present in record-review.sh invocation
  Verify: grep -q -- '--expected-hash' plugins/dso/docs/workflows/REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE.md && grep -q -- '--reviewer-hash' plugins/dso/docs/workflows/REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE.md && echo PASS
- [ ] All required step sections present
  Verify: grep -qE '^## Step [1-5]' plugins/dso/docs/workflows/REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE.md && echo PASS

comments.body: AC amendment: Clarify reviewer-findings.json provenance. Only the deep-tier dso:code-reviewer-* agent in Step 4 may write reviewer-findings.json. Pattern-extraction output from Step 2 must be stored separately (e.g., ARTIFACTS_DIR/huge-pattern-extraction.json) to avoid collision. REVIEWER_HASH in Step 5 comes from the deep-tier synthesis agent output, not from haiku pattern agents. AC: verify workflow distinguishes pattern-extraction storage from reviewer-findings.json.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: AC amendment: Document hash sources explicitly in Step 0 Prerequisites. DIFF_HASH = output of /Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/plugins/dso/hooks/compute-diff-hash.sh (already computed by REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md Step 2, passed through). REVIEWER_HASH = from the deep-tier code-reviewer sub-agent's output in Step 4 (the same source as REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md Step 5). AC: verify workflow documents that --expected-hash DIFF_HASH and --reviewer-hash REVIEWER_HASH are passed to record-review.sh with these specific variable names.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: AC amendment: Throttle handling in Step 2. When effective MAX_AGENTS < 7 (usage throttle), dispatch haiku agents serially (one at a time) rather than routing to FALLBACK. Apply the same ≥5/7 consensus rule regardless of serial vs parallel dispatch. Only route to FALLBACK=THROTTLED when MAX_AGENTS=0 (paused). AC: document this behavior in Step 2.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: AC amendment: Error and cleanup paths. (a) Pattern extraction artifacts stored in ARTIFACTS_DIR/huge/ namespace — cleaned on error or fallback routing. (b) On DIFF_HASH drift at Step 5 (code changed between Step 0 and Step 5), emit HUGE_REVIEW_HASH_DRIFT signal and instruct caller to re-run from REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md Step 2 to recompute the hash. (c) Step 1 INSUFFICIENT_FILES path must clean any partially written artifacts before returning.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: AC amendment: Preserve classifier overlay flags. The security_overlay, performance_overlay, and test_quality_overlay flags computed by review-complexity-classifier.sh on the ORIGINAL full diff must be propagated unchanged to the deep-tier review dispatch in Step 4 for BOTH the CONFIRMED_REFACTOR and FALLBACK paths. Pattern-extraction sampling must NOT suppress overlays. AC: document in Step 4 that overlay flags flow through unmodified.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 2/3 ✓ — Created REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE.md with all required sections
comments.author: Test
